# Resolve activity warnings in Autonomous Ransomware Protection (ARP) before an ONTAP revert![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/notification-icons.svg)The requested article is not available. Either it doesn't apply to this version of the product or the relevant information is organized differently in this version of the docs. You can search, browse, or go back to the other version.![](https://docs.netapp.com/common/2/images/dismiss.svg)# Resolve activity warnings in Autonomous Ransomware Protection (ARP) before an ONTAP revert10/23/2025

Collection of separate PDF docs
```](#)# Creating your file...This may take a few minutes. Thanks for your patience.CancelYour file is readyOKBefore you revert to ONTAP 9.17.1 or earlier, you should respond to any abnormal activity warnings reported by Autonomous Ransomware Protection (ARP) and delete any associated ARP screenshots.Before you beginYou need "Advanced" privileges to delete ARP snapshots.Steps1. Respond to any abnormal activity warnings reported by [ARP](../anti-ransomware/respond-abnormal-task.html) and resolve any potential issues.
2. Confirm the resolution of these issues before reverting by selecting **Update and Clear Suspect File Types** to record your decision and resume normal ARP monitoring.
3. List any ARP screenshots associated with the warnings by running the following command:```
volume snapshot snapshot show -fs-version 9.18
```
4. Delete any ARP screenshots associated with the warnings:| | |
| --- | --- |
| Note | This command deletes all snapshots that have the format used by the current version of ONTAP, potentially not just ARP snapshots. Ensure that you have taken any necessary actions for all snapshots that will be removed before running this command. |```
volume snapshot prepare-for-revert -node <node_name>
```
